title: Fernandoa lutea
taxonomy: Fernandoa lutea is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the golden bird of paradise. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is often cultivated for its vibrant flowers and attractive foliage. Fernandoa lutea is a member of the Apocynaceae family, which includes other species of flowering plants known as oleander and dogbane. The plant is also part of the subfamily Apocynoideae and the genus Fernandoa, which contains only a few species of flowering plants. Fernandoa lutea is classified as an evergreen shrub or small tree, typically growing up to 10 meters in height.
